2013-04-01.title: Ancient Kingdoms in Land of War
2013-04-01.abstract: Sudan’s archaeological record is pivotal to understanding the history of Africa itself, experts say, and a wave of new discoveries may be adding crucial new information.

2013-04-01.title: North Korea Vows to Keep Nuclear Arms and Fix Economy
2013-04-01.abstract: The North’s nuclear weapons “are neither a political bargaining chip nor a thing for economic dealings,” the official Korean Central News Agency quoted officials as saying.

2013-04-01.title: 2 Chinese Cities Move to Cool Overheated Housing Market
2013-04-01.abstract: Beijing and Shanghai announced they would put in place new restrictions and penalties on housing sales to ensure that prices do not continue to soar, fueling what many say is a real estate bubble.

2013-04-01.title: Spurts of Violence Punctuate Calm After Kenyan Vote Is Upheld
2013-04-01.abstract: The clashes that erupted Saturday night after the Supreme Court ruled that Uhuru Kenyatta was legitimately elected president were nowhere near as chaotic as the 2007 disputed election.

2013-04-01.title: Painful Payment for Afghan Debt: A Daughter, 6
2013-04-01.abstract: The story of how a refugee came to trade his daughter to resolve a debt is in part a saga of terrible choices faced by some of the poorest Afghan families.

2013-04-02.title: Double-Digit Inflation Worsens in Iran
2013-04-02.abstract: The government’s announcement of the rising rate appeared to be an acknowledgment of the effects of Western sanctions.

2013-04-02.title: Vote Expected on Arms Sales Treaty
2013-04-02.abstract: The United Nations General Assembly is expected to vote Tuesday on the Arms Trade Treaty, the first global attempt to regulate the $70 billion in annual sales of conventional weapons.

2013-04-02.title: A Slice of London So Exclusive Even the Owners Are Visitors
2013-04-02.abstract: For many recent superwealthy foreign buyers, London is just a stop in a peripatetic international existence that might also include New York, Moscow and Monaco.

2013-04-02.title: American Woman Gang-Raped and Beaten on Brazilian Transit Van
2013-04-02.abstract: The harrowing assault stunned many in Brazil, especially as Rio de Janeiro tries to promote itself as a city on the mend and prepares to host the 2014 World Cup and 2016 Olympic Games.

2013-04-02.title: Why’s That Taxi on the Stairs? ‘You’re in Odessa’
2013-04-02.abstract: On April Fools’ Day, the Ukrainian city weighed down by history celebrates its tradition of wry humor and charming scoundrels, a practice that began 40 years ago.

2013-04-03.title: Egyptians Struggle as Wary Tourists Stay Away
2013-04-03.abstract: Every headline about a riot deepens the post-uprising crisis for Egypt’s tourism trade, a problem no amount of salesmanship by officials seems able to fix.

2013-04-03.title: France: Ex-Minister Admits Lying About Secret Bank Account
2013-04-03.abstract: The former budget minister, Jérôme Cahuzac, admitted Tuesday that he had lied to Parliament and the president about the existence of a secret overseas bank account.

2013-04-03.title: Brazil: Suspects in Gang Rape Linked to Assaults and Robberies
2013-04-03.abstract: The Rio de Janeiro police said Tuesday they were investigating a new claim of rape against three men arrested in connection with the rape of an American student in a transit van.

2013-04-03.title: In Mexican Villages, Few Are Left to Dream of U.S.
2013-04-03.abstract: Some American lawmakers worry that a pathway to citizenship could encourage illegal immigration, but in some parts of Mexico there seem to be few people remaining to come.

2013-04-03.title: Empty Mexican Villages Raise Doubts on New Immigration Wave
2013-04-03.abstract: American lawmakers worry that  a pathway to citizenship could encourage illegal immigration, but in Mexico, there seem to be few left to come.

2013-04-04.title: Power Struggle Is Gripping Iran Ahead of June Election
2013-04-04.abstract: With only months to go in his last presidential term, Mahmoud Ahmadinejad has surprised many of his critics by challenging a coalition of traditionalists.

2013-04-04.title: Multiplying the Old Divisions of Class in Britain
2013-04-04.abstract: Using an online questionnaire filled out by over 161,000 people, the BBC has concluded that in today’s complicated world, there are seven social classes, not three.

2013-04-04.title: Attacks on Elderly Armenian Women in Turkey Awaken Fears
2013-04-04.abstract: The violence recalls a tortured past and, perhaps, hints at future tensions as Turkey prepares to face the 100th anniversary of the Armenian genocide.

2013-04-04.title: Greece: Court Sentences Guerrillas
2013-04-04.abstract: A Greek court on Wednesday handed down 50-year prison sentences to three leading members of the guerrilla group Revolutionary Struggle.

2013-04-04.title: Becoming a Female Police Officer in Delhi: Do You Qualify?
2013-04-04.abstract: As Delhi’s police force recruits thousands of women, a look at what it takes to get hired.

2013-04-05.title: French Uproar on Secret Assets Escalates
2013-04-05.abstract: Officials rejected accusations that President François Hollande might have been aware of the secret foreign bank accounts of an ex-budget minister and a campaign treasurer.

2013-04-05.title: South Africa to Withdraw Its Troops From Central African Republic
2013-04-05.abstract: President Jacob Zuma is bringing his troops home after at least 13 soldiers were killed last month in a firefight with rebels who overthrew the Central African Republic’s president.

2013-04-05.title: ‘Tomb Sweeping’ Festival in China
2013-04-05.abstract: Large crowds flock to their relatives’ graves to clean away debris and leave behind temporal favorites of the deceased.

2013-04-05.title: Ethnic Rifts Strain Myanmar as It Moves Toward Democracy
2013-04-05.abstract: A visit to the Kachin region is a sobering reminder of how much hatred and mistrust exist between the majority Burman and the ethnic minorities who live in the country’s highlands.

2013-04-05.title: Beijing Flaunts Cross-Border Clout in Search for Drug Lord
2013-04-05.abstract: The manhunt that led to a notorious trafficker’s capture was a hard-nosed display of the Chinese government’s political and economic sway across Laos, Myanmar and Thailand.

2013-04-06.title: As Brazil’s Influence Expands, So Does a Campaign Strategist’s Success
2013-04-06.abstract: João Santana, a hypercompetitive former journalist, exemplifies the growing influence of Brazilian political consultants in Latin America.

2013-04-06.title: Detecting Shift, U.S. Makes Case to China on North Korea
2013-04-06.abstract: The Obama administration, seeing signs of Chinese frustration toward Pyongyang, is pressing China to crack down or face more of an American military presence in Asia.

2013-04-06.title: U.N. Says It Is Running Out of Money to Assist Wave of Refugees From Syria
2013-04-06.abstract: A Unicef official says that with 1.25 million Syrians registered as refugees, “the needs are rising exponentially and we are broke.”

2013-04-06.title: Editor Who Wrote of Racism in Cuba Loses His Post, Colleagues Say
2013-04-06.abstract: The New York Times stood by the preparation of an opinion piece that Roberto Zurbano wrote before being removed from his post as an editor of a Cuban publishing house.

2013-04-06.title: IHT Quick Read: April 6
2013-04-06.abstract: Investor concern on Korea; advances on exam grading; arrest shows China’s reach; and U.S. overhauls food aid.

2013-04-07.title: Edith Schaeffer, Definer of Christian Family Values, Dies at 98
2013-04-07.abstract: Mrs. Schaeffer wrote books describing women’s domestic role in an increasingly secular world, and with her husband, founded the Christian commune L’Abri.

2013-04-07.title: Kerry Moves to Help Turkey and Israel to Restore Ties
2013-04-07.abstract: The ties of Turkey and Israel have been frozen since 2010, but Secretary of State John Kerry is to meet with Turkey’s premier to discuss an agreement between the nations.

2013-04-07.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2013-04-07.abstract: Best of long-form journalism this week.

2013-04-07.title: Afghan Children Reported Among Dead in U.S. Strike
2013-04-07.abstract: The latest civilian casualties in eastern Afghanistan added to a particularly deadly week of fighting.

2013-04-07.title: South Korea Expects Missile Launch by North
2013-04-07.abstract: Tensions on the Korea peninsula remain high with a spokesman in Seoul saying the South expected a missile launch later this week.

2013-04-08.title: Attack on Christians in Egypt Comes After a Pledge
2013-04-08.abstract: The third day of sectarian violence in Egypt tested the pledge of the nation’s Islamist president, Mohamed Morsi, to protect the Christian minority.

2013-04-08.title: U.S. Designs a Korea Response Proportional to the Provocation
2013-04-08.abstract: American officials said new “counterprovocation” plans called for a response that would hit the source of any attack by North Korea with similar weapons.

2013-04-08.title: Targeted Killing Comes to Define War on Terror
2013-04-08.abstract: Despite administration officials’ statements that their strong preference is for capturing terrorists, a reliance on killing terrorism suspects has defined the Obama years.

2013-04-08.title: Negotiators Find in Kazakhstan the Perfect Place to Disagree
2013-04-08.abstract: Talks over Iran’s nuclear program ended with no deal, but negotiators did agree on one point: Kazakhstan’s “flawless performance” as host.

2013-04-08.title: Small Leak Is Reported at Fukushima Nuclear Plant
2013-04-08.abstract: The plant’s operator, the Tokyo Electric Power Company, said that just over three quarts of water was believed to have leaked from the plant’s No. 3 storage pool.

2013-04-09.title: Kerry to Focus on Palestinian Economy as Part of Peace Process
2013-04-09.abstract: As he continued his trip to the Middle East, Secretary of State John Kerry met with the Palestinian prime minister and Israeli president and prime minister.

2013-04-09.title: Even in Death, Chávez Is a Powerful Presence
2013-04-09.abstract: As Nicolás Maduro invokes Hugo Chávez’s ghost at rallies,  Henrique Capriles Radonski, Mr. Maduro’s opponent in Sunday’s presidential vote, is wooing rivals of the late leader.

2013-04-09.title: Trip to Cuba by Beyoncé and Jay-Z Is Investigated
2013-04-09.abstract: Miami-Havana tensions are being stoked as two Florida lawmakers seek an inquiry into possible trade embargo violations by the musicians.

2013-04-09.title: Judge Rules Against Veteran Who Fought Alongside Syrian Rebels
2013-04-09.abstract: During an appearance in federal court in Alexandria, Va., Eric G. Harroun was denied release to home detention in Arizona.

2013-04-09.abstract: James Wasserstrom, whose protracted legal battle with the United Nations ended a nearly 30-year career there, has asked Secretary of State John Kerry for help.

2013-04-10.title: Peace Envoys From Taliban at Loose Ends in Qatar

2013-04-10.title: Compromise Is Proposed on Western Wall Praying
2013-04-10.abstract: People seeking to pray at the holy site in Jerusalem in less traditional fashion would have a separate area of their own.

2013-04-10.title: France’s Chief Rabbi Declines to Resign Over Plagiarism
2013-04-10.abstract: Rabbi Gilles Bernheim, who acknowledged plagiarizing from other writers for a book on Jewish meditations, said that to resign “would be an act of vanity and desertion.”

2013-04-10.title: Amnesty International Reports on Death Penalty Trends
2013-04-10.abstract: Worldwide, fewer people were given a death sentence in 2012 compared with 2011, but about the same number of executions were carried out.

2013-04-10.title: Cuba Trip by Star Pair Was Legal, Official Says
2013-04-10.abstract: Jay-Z and Beyoncé were part of a fully licensed trip to Cuba two weeks ago, the Treasury Department said Tuesday in response to a query by two Florida lawmakers.

2013-04-11.abstract: Germany’s Central Office for the Investigation of Nazi Crimes compiled a list of 50 former guards who worked at the concentration camp in Poland and might be brought to court.

2013-04-11.abstract: An event organized by the Serbian president of the General Assembly appeared to be a thinly disguised complaint forum about a war-crimes tribunal for the former Yugoslavia.

2013-04-11.abstract: The United States is poised to increase its nonlethal aid to opposition groups as pressure builds to lift a European Union embargo on sending arms to Syria.

2013-04-11.abstract: A deadly prison raid by soldiers has raised questions about what progress has been made in overhauling the military since the collapse of President Suharto’s rule in 1998.

2013-04-11.abstract: Recent mishaps at Fukushima — including a blackout triggered by a dead rat — have underscored just how vulnerable the site is to leaks, breakdowns and simple human error.

2013-04-12.title: France: Chief Rabbi Steps Down Over Plagiarism
2013-04-12.abstract: The chief rabbi of France, Gilles Bernheim, apologized Thursday after admitting to plagiarism in at least two of his books and lying about his academic credentials.

2013-04-12.abstract: The defiance of Murat Karayilan, commander of the Kurdistan Workers’ Party, suggests that the process could be longer and more arduous than anticipated.

2013-04-12.abstract: The immigration judge’s ruling ordered the deportation of former Gen. Carlos Eugenio Vides Casanova for his role in human rights violations, including 1980 rape and murder of four American churchwomen.

2013-04-12.abstract: The Treasury Department said Babak Morteza Zanjani had conspired with a bank and a network of front companies to move money for Iran.

2013-04-12.abstract: After the president of Argentina, Cristina Fernández de Kirchner, did not receive an invitation to the ceremonial funeral for Margaret Thatcher in London next week, Argentina called it “yet another provocation.”

2013-04-13.title: Syrian Rebels Break With Group Over Qaeda Wing Alliance
2013-04-13.abstract: A coalition of insurgents criticized a more radical group’s decision to team up with Al Qaeda’s Iraq branch as a mistake that would benefit only their enemy, President Bashar al-Assad.

2013-04-13.abstract: A sudden round of finger-pointing in the American intelligence community this week also highlighted the sensitivity surrounding nuclear threats in the wake of failures in Iraq.

2013-04-13.abstract: As the crisis with North Korea escalates, critics and supporters alike wonder if President Park Geun-hye may have gone too far in presenting herself as an ultra-tough leader.

2013-04-13.abstract: As long-awaited elections approach, faith is slipping in Morgan Tsvangirai’s Movement for Democratic Change, which now governs alongside President Robert Mugabe.

2013-04-13.abstract: As elections near, some voters who once had faith in Morgan Tsvangirai’s opposition party ask if it has been tainted by governing with Robert Mugabe.

2013-04-14.title: China Makes Inroads in Nepal, and Stanches Tibetan Influx
2013-04-14.abstract: The Chinese are trying to stem the flow of disaffected Tibetans fleeing to Nepal and to enlist the help of the Nepalese in cracking down on the political activities of those already there.

2013-04-14.abstract: Psy, the Korean pop sensation and creator of “Gangnam Style,” referred to North Korea during his Seoul concert on Saturday, amid some comic comparisons between him and the North Korean leader and some speculation he could be a peacemaker.

2013-04-14.abstract: The strife-torn Aceh Province passed a bylaw last month that declared the banner of a former armed separatist movement to be the province’s official flag and seal.

2013-04-14.title: Venezuela Goes to the Polls

2013-04-14.title: Kerry Says North Korea Talks Are Possible, but Hints at Conditions
2013-04-14.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry said that the United States was prepared to reach out if North Korea made the first move to abandon its nuclear weapons program.

2013-04-15.title: Venezuela Gives Chávez Protégé Narrow Victory
2013-04-15.abstract: Nicolás Maduro, the handpicked protégé of Hugo Chávez, won an unexpectedly close race for the presidency. His rival demanded a recount.

2013-04-15.abstract: The Brahmaputra River, a critical laboratory in climate studies, is fast eroding an island in Assam that it has encircled and sustained for ages.

2013-04-15.abstract: Afghans who worked for American forces have found themselves without military protection and facing a backlog for visas that could help give them protection.

2013-04-15.abstract: Many spoke of the horrors they endured as children, when the government was pursuing leftist guerrillas and anyone believed to be a sympathizer.

2013-04-15.abstract: Ms. Weinstein was the driving force behind the restoration of Maimonides’s yeshiva and other monuments of Egyptian Jewish history.

2013-04-16.title: U.S. Sting That Snared African Ex-Admiral Shines Light on Drug Trade
2013-04-16.abstract: The former chief of the Guinea-Bissau Navy, who was labeled a “drug kingpin” by American officials, faced trafficking charges Monday after a D.E.A. sting.

2013-04-16.abstract: Preservationists say illegal demolitions, chronic underinvestment and unimaginative architecture threaten to erase the city’s history.

2013-04-16.title: Under Orders, French Officials Reveal Financial Worth
2013-04-16.abstract: President François Hollande ordered his 38 cabinet ministers to publicly declare their holdings as part of an effort to “moralize” French politics.

2013-04-16.abstract: No longer treated with profound deference, King Juan Carlos and the rest of the monarchy are under scrutiny, and some residents are even calling for an elected head of state.

2013-04-16.abstract: Loyalists to President Bashar al-Assad of Syria breached the blockage of two important bases, possibly opening the way to try to recapture lost territory.

2013-04-17.abstract: Wednesday’s funeral rites for Margaret Thatcher, the former British prime minister, have become a matter of dispute in Whitwell, where many lost their jobs during the 1980s coal battles.

2013-04-17.abstract: The authorities in Belgium raided 48 homes nationwide and arrested suspects in what prosecutors described as a jihadist recruitment drive for the insurgency in Syria.

2013-04-17.abstract: The ceremonial funeral with military honors for Margaret Thatcher has divided British opinion, much as the former prime minister stirred passions in her lifetime.

2013-04-17.title: What Foreign Investors Want to Hear From India
2013-04-17.abstract: Advice from a foreign investor, as P. Chidambaram, India’s finance minister, travels to North America to stump for investment.

2013-04-17.title: IHT Quick Read: April 17
2013-04-17.abstract: The investigation into the Boston Marathon bombing continues; protests in Venezuela after the election; Europe votes to cap banker bonuses; and reviews of London theater and Zurich opera productions.

2013-04-18.title: More Children in Greece Are Going Hungry
2013-04-18.abstract: Greece’s economic free fall is reshaping the lives of families with children, many of whom arrive at school hungry, underfed or even malnourished.

2013-04-18.abstract: A panel of former senior American officials and outside experts asked President Obama to reconsider the likelihood that harsh sanctions will drive Tehran to concessions.

2013-04-18.title: No Deal Yet in Talks to Reduce Strife Between Serbia and Kosovo
2013-04-18.abstract: The countries have agreed on the broad principle that Serbs in a Serbian-majority area in northern Kosovo would gain more powers and tacitly recognize Kosovo’s government.

2013-04-18.abstract: European Union members reached a preliminary agreement as part of an effort to make opposition-held areas self-sufficient, diplomats said.

2013-04-18.title: Honduras: Attorney General Is Suspended
2013-04-18.abstract: Saying it was frustrated with some of the worst criminal violence in the world, the Honduran congress suspended the attorney general and his assistant.

2013-04-19.title: U.S. Indicts Guinea-Bissau’s Military Chief in Drug Case
2013-04-19.abstract: Gen. Antonio Injai was charged in New York on cocaine and weapons charges, part of an operation targeting top figures in a country long considered a major narco-state.

2013-04-19.abstract: In critical remarks, a spokesman for the Afghan president said that an airstrike in which 12 children were killed was part of a C.I.A.-run operation.

2013-04-19.title: New Details on Disclosure Regarding North Korea
2013-04-19.abstract: An assessment of North Korea’s nuclear capabilities was mistakenly declassified and released, but it did not reflect a consensus view, the director of national intelligence said.

2013-04-19.title: Polish Museum Repairs a Tie to a Jewish Past
2013-04-19.abstract: Among civic leaders in Warsaw, a new Jewish museum is seen as a major step toward recognizing Poland’s Jewish past and recovering from its 20th-century traumas.

2013-04-19.abstract: A $10 billion deal would provide missiles, warplanes and troop transports to help Israel, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates counter any future threat from Iran.

2013-04-20.title: Study Finds Sharp Rise in Attacks by Taliban
2013-04-20.abstract: It is unclear whether militants are able to mount an all-out attempt to test the Afghan security forces as they take over from withdrawing allies.

2013-04-20.abstract: Nicolás Maduro won the election by a narrow margin, but his opponent, Henrique Capriles Radonski, has refused to recognize the result.

2013-04-20.title: In a Father’s Political Footsteps, but More Uphill
2013-04-20.abstract: Justin Trudeau became the leader of the Liberal Party in Canada on Sunday, 45 years after his father, Pierre Elliott Trudeau, claimed a similar victory. But the parallels may end there.

2013-04-20.title: Surviving China’s Latest Earthquake, but Afraid to Go Home
2013-04-20.abstract: The earthquake struck the western region of Sichuan Province, where a previous quake killed 70,000 people.

2013-04-20.title: Rape of 5-Year-Old Girl Sets Off New Furor in India
2013-04-20.abstract: An attack and the police response struck a chord in the nation, still dealing with the trauma of another brutal rape case.

2013-04-21.title: Danes Rethink a Welfare State Ample to a Fault
2013-04-21.abstract: With little fuss or political protest — or notice abroad — Denmark has been at work overhauling entitlements, trying to prod Danes into working more, longer or both.

2013-04-21.title: Kerry Says U.S. Will Double Aid to Rebels in Syria
2013-04-21.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry said the $123 million in new aid would be used to provide “nonlethal” supplies to the military wing of the Syrian opposition.

2013-04-21.abstract: With their daughter’s death in the Boston Marathon attack, the parents of Lu Lingzi joined a growing group in China — parents who lose a single child in a country that mandates one child for many.

2013-04-21.title: Hagel, in Israel, Presses U.S. Agenda on Deterring Iran
2013-04-21.abstract: Defense Secretary Chuck Hagel argued for patience in dealing with Iran but emphasized Israel’s right to self-defense.

2013-04-21.title: Kerry Asks Turkish Leader to Postpone Trip to Gaza
2013-04-21.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry expressed concern that a visit by Turkey’s leader could harm efforts to restart the Israeli-Palestinian peace process.

2013-04-22.title: Activist’s Death in Egypt Spurs Charges of Police Abuse
2013-04-22.abstract: The death of Mohamed el-Gindy has underscored one of the most vexing challenges facing the new government: how to tame Egypt’s unaccountable and despised security forces.

2013-04-22.title: Conservative Tobacco Magnate Wins Presidential Race in Paraguay
2013-04-22.abstract: Electoral officials declared Horacio Cartes of the Colorado Party the winner after voters seemed ready to give Mr. Cartes the benefit of the doubt over claims of  drug trafficking and other charges leveled against him.

2013-04-22.abstract: Tamerlan Tsarnaev, one of the suspects in the Boston bombings, found comfort in Dagestan, a region in the North Caucasus that is the center of violent Islamic insurgency in Russia.

2013-04-22.abstract: Beppe Grillo of the Five Star Movement organized supporters to express dismay at the tactics behind the election that assured a second term for President Giorgio Napolitano.

2013-04-22.abstract: A report by a human rights group accuses Myanmar’s government of standing by, or worse, as Buddhists conducted a campaign of violence against Muslims.

2013-04-23.title: German Soccer Hero Faces Prison Amid Record Run and Scandal
2013-04-23.abstract: Uli Hoeness, a former soccer champion, faces up to 10 years in prison after he acknowledged that he kept a secret bank account to evade taxes.

2013-04-23.title: China: Prison for Preachers
2013-04-23.abstract: Chinese authorities have sentenced to prison seven underground church leaders arrested last April and charged with belonging to an illegal cult, according to a Christian rights group.

2013-04-23.title: Old Sore Spots Flare Up in China-Japan Disputes
2013-04-23.abstract: Lawmakers in Tokyo visited a shrine whose symbolism has angered China, while the two nations squared off over contested islands.

2013-04-23.title: When ‘Love Marriage’ Needs a Little Help
2013-04-23.abstract: Forward-looking Indian parents, who agree not to force children into arranged marriages, push kids to wed in other ways.

2013-04-23.title: China’s Criticism of U.S. on Human Rights Draws Support (and Mockery)
2013-04-23.abstract: Many Chinese netizens were unimpressed by a state report criticizing the U.S. human rights situation, which focused on gun violence, among other things.

2013-04-24.title: Fearing Price Increases, Iranians Hoard Goods

2013-04-24.title: New Threat in Nigeria as Militants Split Off
2013-04-24.abstract: The Islamist group Ansaru, an offshoot of Boko Haram, says it eschews the killing of fellow Nigerians and prefers kidnapping and killing foreigners.

2013-04-24.title: Drone Strikes Turn Allies Into Enemies, Yemeni Says
2013-04-24.abstract: In testimony before a Senate Judiciary subcommittee, a Yemeni man who had studied in the United States said the strikes turned Yemenis against America.

2013-04-24.title: Canada: Suspects Appear in Court Over Charges in Derailment Plot
2013-04-24.abstract: The two men charged with plotting to derail a Canadian passenger train with the assistance of Al Qaeda denied the charges in court appearances on Tuesday.

2013-04-24.abstract: A British businessman who sold the Iraqi security forces fake bomb detectors based on $20 golf ball finders bought from the United States was convicted of fraud on Tuesday in Britain.

2013-04-25.title: Building Collapse Near Dhaka, Bangladesh
2013-04-25.abstract: The collapse of an eight-story building in Bangladesh that housed several garment factories injured hundreds of people.

2013-04-25.title: Boom Times in Paraguay Leave Many Behind
2013-04-25.abstract: On paper, Paraguay is the fastest-growing country in the Americas, but its economic success exists only in pockets: more than 30 percent of the population lives in poverty.

2013-04-25.title:
U.S. Says It
Suspects Assad
Used Chemical
Weapons
2013-04-25.abstract: The White House said on Thursday that U.S. intelligence agencies now assess, with “varying degrees of confidence,” that the Syrian government has used chemical weapons.

2013-04-25.title: As Bird Flu Spreads to Taiwan, Governments Act to Prepare
2013-04-25.abstract: A man in Taiwan has contracted the H7N9 virus, and officials in the region are taking precautions to prevent spread to their countries.

2013-04-25.title: Boston Attack Spotlights Struggle Half a World Away
2013-04-25.abstract: How the situation in the Russian republic of Dagestan, where religious strife and anti-Russian feelings fuel relentless violence, may have played a role in a terrorist attack in Boston.

2013-04-26.title: U.N. Votes to Establish Peacekeeping Force for Mali
2013-04-26.abstract: Despite fears about getting embroiled in the fight against Islamist extremists, the Security Council on Thursday established a force for Mali, building on a recent military intervention by the French.

2013-04-26.title: Mexican Teacher Protests Turn Up Heat on President
2013-04-26.abstract: Members of the teachers’ union in Guerrero State showed their anger, and in some cases turned violent, over President Enrique Peña Nieto’s plan to overhaul the education system.

2013-04-26.title: Venezuela Says U.S. Citizen Plotted Unrest
2013-04-26.abstract: A United States citizen was arrested and accused of seeking to provoke violence in Venezuela after the recent presidential election.

2013-04-26.title: Kurdish Rebel Group to Withdraw From Turkey
2013-04-26.abstract: The main Kurdish rebel group said it would withdraw all of its forces from Turkey by May 8 to end a 30-year conflict with the Turkish state.

2013-04-26.title: Cuba: U.S. Bars Raúl Castro’s Daughter From a Forum
2013-04-26.abstract: The State Department denied the daughter of President Raúl Castro of Cuba permission to attend a gay rights conference in Philadelphia, where she was to receive an award next week.

2013-04-27.title: Frenchman Will Return to China Prized Bronze Artifacts Looted in 19th Century
2013-04-27.abstract: The offer to return the objects came from one of 60 high-powered Frenchmen accompanying President François Hollande on his first visit to China.

2013-04-27.title: Israel Sees U.S. Response to Syria as Gauge on Iran
2013-04-27.abstract: Israeli leaders say they are watching President Obama’s response to Syria’s apparent use of chemical weapons for clues about how he might handle the Iranian nuclear issue.

2013-04-27.title: Europe Facing More Pressure to Reconsider Cuts as a Cure
2013-04-27.abstract: European officials who favored austerity have adopted a softer tone, backing away from a strategy that is widely unpopular with the public.

2013-04-27.title: U.S. Filmmaker Held in Venezuela Sought to Show Political Divide, Friends Say
2013-04-27.abstract: Tim Tracy, who was arrested on Wednesday, is accused of plotting to destabilize Venezuela and promoting civil war.

2013-04-27.title: In Trek North, First Lure Is Mexico’s Other Line
2013-04-27.abstract: Central Americans fleeing crime and economic stagnation are flowing freely across Mexico’s southern border and causing a spike in migration to the United States.

2013-04-28.title: Who Rules the Street in Cairo? The Residents Who Build It
2013-04-28.abstract: In the wake of their revolution, Egyptians must determine for themselves what their capital should look like: not just politically and socially, but also architecturally.

2013-04-28.title: The Egyptian Revolution’s Transformation of Cairo, Block by Block
2013-04-28.abstract: After decades of not being able to make decisions about their city, Cairenes are taking its development into their own hands.

2013-04-28.title: Confidence Issues Linger as the Dreamliner Soars Again
2013-04-28.abstract: Boeing’s Dreamliner is back in the air, but questions linger about the cause of the battery overheating, the technology fixes – and confidence.

2013-04-28.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2013-04-28.abstract: Best of long-form journalism this week.

2013-04-28.title: 3 Shot Near Premier’s Office as Italy’s Cabinet Is Sworn In
2013-04-28.abstract: The shooting in Rome was shown live by a state broadcaster that had a television crew in front of Prime Minister Enrico Letta’s office.

2013-04-29.title: Clashes in Iraq Carry Worries of a New Civil War
2013-04-29.abstract: After the violence last week, Iraqis are asking two questions: Is the country headed toward a new civil war? And, if so, will a group of former Baathists lead one side of it?

2013-04-29.title: Tears and Rage as Hope Fades in Bangladesh
2013-04-29.abstract: The collapse of a building, the Rana Plaza, is considered the deadliest accident in the history of the garment industry, having claimed at least 377 lives.

2013-04-29.title: New Look at a C.I.A. Officer’s Death
2013-04-29.abstract: Two decades after Freddie Woodruff was killed by a single shot to the head in Georgia, the former Soviet republic, the justice minister said the case had not been properly investigated.

2013-04-29.title: Greek Parliament Passes Plan for Layoffs
2013-04-29.abstract: The contentious plan would dismiss 15,000 civil servants by the end of next year as part of a new package of economic measures.

2013-04-29.title: Russian Security Forces Kill 2 Suspected Rebels in Dagestan
2013-04-29.abstract: Officials said one of the men had been behind a series of bomb attacks on the police.

2013-04-30.title: Steering France’s Economy, and Attacked From All Sides
2013-04-30.abstract: The finance minister, Pierre Moscovici, is attacked from the right for not being firm enough in cutting public spending, and from the left for being too willing to cut public spending in a period of stagnation.

2013-04-30.title: 7 Killed in Plane Crash in  Afghanistan
2013-04-30.abstract: The civilian cargo plane of a military contractor based in New York crashed at Bagram Air Base, northeast of Kabul, shortly after takeoff.

2013-04-30.title: Flow of Tainted Water Is Latest Crisis at Japan Nuclear Plant
2013-04-30.abstract: Workers at the Fukushima Daiichi power plant were struggling to contain groundwater that was pouring into the plant’s reactor buildings by the minute.

2013-04-30.title: A City That Turns Garbage Into Energy Copes With a Shortage
2013-04-30.abstract: Oslo, where roughly half the city and most of its schools are heated by burning garbage, is forced to import garbage to supply its waste-to-energy incinerating plants.

2013-04-30.title: Italy’s New Premier Puts Stimulus First
2013-04-30.abstract: Prime Minister Enrico Letta said he would move quickly to stimulate growth and jobs, while easing some austerity measures enacted during the euro zone crisis.
